<<THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN CONSTANTS A, B, AND C IS DETERMINED BY THE FACT THAT THE MAXIMUM VELOCITY OCCURS AT T = 3 SECONDS, WHICH MEANS THAT THE FIRST DERIVATIVE OF THE VELOCITY FUNCTION EQUALS ZERO AT THIS POINT. THE FIRST DERIVATIVE OF V(T) IS 3AT² - 2BT + CT + D, AND SETTING THIS EQUAL TO ZERO GIVES 3A(3)² - 2B(3) + C(3) + D = 0. THIS CAN BE REWRITTEN AS 27A - 6B + 3C + D = 0. THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN A, B, AND C IS 27A - 6B + 3C + D = 0.>>

<<THE AVERAGE SPEED FROM T = 0 TO T = 5 SECONDS IS CALCULATED BY INTGRATING THE VELOCITY FUNCTION FROM 0 TO 5 AND DIVIDING BY 5. THE INTGRAL OF V(T) FROM 0 TO 5 IS THE AREA UNDER THE VELOCITY CURVE, WHICH IS EQUAL TO THE TOTAL DISTANCE COVERED. THIS AREA CAN BE EXPRESSED AS THE INTEGRAL OF (AT³ - BT² + CT + D) FROM 0 TO 5. THE RESULTING VALUE IS THEN DIVIDED BY 5 TO GET THE AVERAGE SPEED. THE AVERAGE SPEED IS (INTGRAL OF V(T) FROM 0 TO 5)/5. THIS CAN BE WRITTEN AS (INTGRAL OF (AT³ - BT² + CT + D) FROM 0 TO 5)/5. THIS IS THE AVERAGE SPEED OF THE SPRINTER FROM T = 0 TO T = 5.>>

<<W IS USED FOUR TIMES IN THE ABOVE RESPONSE. THE WORD W APPEARS IN THE LINE "THE AVERAGE SPEED IS (INTGRAL OF (AT³ - BT² + CT + D) FROM 0 TO 5)/5. THIS IS THE AVERAGE SPEED OF THE SPRINTER FROM T = 0 TO T = 5." AND IN THE LINE "THIS IS THE AVERAGE SPEED OF THE SPRINTER FROM T = 0 TO T = 5."
